nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; tends to rest on, in, or beneath stony and soft corals in clear lagoon, channel, and seaward reef habitats at depths of roughly 1–40 m (typically 10–25 m).

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on small crustaceans (shrimp, crabs, mysis, krill, artemia) and small fishes. Generally accepts a varied diet of frozen meaty marine preparations and, less ideally, live feeder shrimp in captivity; small portions delivered two to three times daily are commonly advised.

summary: A small but pugnacious Indo-Pacific hawkfish with a whitish body and red spots; generally considered easy on water quality but aggressive toward small tankmates and an accomplished jumper that needs a tightly covered tank.
careNotes:
The Coral Hawkfish (Cirrhitichthys oxycephalus) is widely sold under the trade names 'Pixy Hawkfish' and 'Spotted Hawkfish', referring to the same biological species. It is typically described as having a whitish to pale-blue body marked with large red or red-brown spots. Specimens have been reported to perch motionlessly on coral branches, rockwork, or the substrate, ambushing prey rather than actively swimming — a behavior linked to a reduced or absent swimbladder that tends to make them 'hop' rather than swim conventionally.

This species is generally considered reef-safe with caution: corals are typically left undisturbed, but ornamental shrimp (cleaner shrimp, peppermint shrimp), small hermit crabs, and small fish are at significant risk of predation. It is commonly advised to keep only one hawkfish per tank and to add it last, as it has been known to harass new tankmates aggressively, including fish considerably larger than itself. Bonded pairs are occasionally reported when introduced simultaneously, but this is not the norm.

This species is an accomplished jumper that has been observed darting upward with sudden bursts of speed; a tightly fitting lid or mesh cover is generally considered essential. FishBase records the species as territorial and haremic in the wild, with males presiding over a small group of females, and as a pelagic spawner with brief spawning ascents into the water column.

Cross-source disagreements: adult size estimates range from ~7.6 cm (LiveAquaria's typical sold size) through ~10 cm (Aquarium Wiki, Fishkeeper, ReefApp) up to a recorded maximum of 15.2 cm SL on FishBase; the 8–12 cm range used here reflects the commonly attained adult size in aquaria, with the FishBase maximum noted as a wild upper bound. Minimum tank size sources also diverged: LiveAquaria suggests 30 gal (~114 L) and ReefApp 100 L, while Fishkeeping World/community sources recommend ~300 L when housed with other fish. Given its territorial and aggressive temperament, ambush-perch lifestyle, and need for both open perching/swimming space and rocky shelter, 200 L has been adopted as a cautious minimum that biases toward the larger end without demanding the full 300 L community recommendation. Temperature ranges spanned 22–28 °C across sources; the 23–27 °C window used here averages those figures.

The care level has been recorded as 'Easy' (LiveAquaria) and 'Hardy / Suitable with care' (ReefApp); the intermediate rating used here reflects the need to manage its aggression, ensure a securely covered tank, and select compatible tankmates rather than any particular sensitivity to water quality. Hawkfishes in this genus are generally documented as protogynous (female-first) hermaphrodites.
breedingNotes: Not commonly bred in captivity. Wild spawning has been documented at dusk in pelagic fashion, with mated pairs ascending 0.3–1.0 m into the water column to release gametes.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Species product page. Provided size (3 in / 7.6 cm), tank size (30 gal / 114 L), temperature (72–78°F), pH (8.1–8.4), SG (1.020–1.025), dKH (8–12), care level (Easy), temperament (Aggressive), reef compatibility (With Caution). Reached via the LiveAquaria hawkfish collection root.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Cirrhitidae, Centrarchiformes), maximum length 15.2 cm SL, depth 1–40 m (typical 10–25 m), wild temperature 24–28°C, Indo-Pacific + Eastern Pacific distribution, carnivorous diet (crustaceans and small fishes), territorial/haremic behavior, and pelagic spawning.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Secondary cross-check. Confirmed adult size ~10 cm, minimum tank ~100 L, temperature ~26°C, SG ~1.026, carnivore, reef-safe with caution, hardy/suitable with care. Noted possibility of bonded-pair coexistence if introduced simultaneously. Reef2Reef wiki (designated first secondary in source-urls.json) returned no species-specific page; FishBase used as the primary secondary instead.

additional.name: Fishkeeper
additional.notes: Third cross-check. Confirmed adult size 10 cm, temperature 24–28°C, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, territorial 'confident and pugnacious' temperament, reef safety with caution. Independently documented the jumping behavior and recommendation for a covered tank, and noted incompatibility with dottybacks, firefish, and gobies.
